SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.descriptor

Interface ClassDescriptor

All Known Implementing Classes:
EntityClassImpl
public interface ClassDescriptor
Class to hold the metadata of a JPA entity.

At the time of initializing the managed classes, the metadata of these classes are extracted and kept the in the ClassDescriptor. Later this metadata is used to build various queries to facilitate communicaation with the DB. This is a crucial part of framework initialization. If an entity/managed class is wrongly configured, then the application bootstrap will fail. Therefore utmost care must be taken while modelling the jpa entities.

JPA-LiTE does not support all attributes of persistence framework. All sort of mapping attributes, e.g., OneToMany, ManyToOne, ManyToMany are ignored. This is purposely done to increase the throughput of the application. Traditionaal jpa framework, e.g., Eclipselink, Hibernaate, ObjectDB, etc are slow. And that's because of unnecessary join attributes. Hence using these attributes is strongly discouraged.

Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    Return the attribute object for this attribute/field name.

    Return the table name that is represented by this entity class.

  • Method Details

    • entityClass

      Class<?> entityClass()
      Return the java type of the entity/managed class. The identification criteria of an entity is Entity annotation. If no such annotation is present, then the framework will fail to initialize.
      Returns:
      Class

    • table

      String table()
      Return the table name that is represented by this entity class. The table definition is obtained from Table annotation.
      Returns:
      String

    • attributeCount

      int attributeCount()
      Return the number of attributes this entity has.
      Returns:
      int

    • idCount

      int idCount()
      Return the column count representing the primary key.
      Returns:
      int

    • primaryKey

      Class<?> primaryKey()
      Return the java type of the primary key. Because it is advisable all tables to have a primary key, therefore, it is mandatory for all entity/managed class to have a primary key attribute. It can be a composite key or a single primary key. In either case the primary key class must be associated with the entity class via IdClass annotation.
      Returns:
      Class

    • attribute

      EntityAttribute attribute(String name)
      Return the attribute object for this attribute/field name.
      Parameters:
      name - Name of the java field
      Returns:
      EntityAttribute

    • hasAttribute

      boolean hasAttribute(String name)
      Check if the entity class has an attribute as identified by the column name.
      Parameters:
      name - Column name.
      Returns:
      boolean

    • attributes

      Iterator<EntityAttribute> attributes()
      Return an iterator over the set of attributes that this entity has.
      Returns:
      Iterator

    • ids

      Iterator<EntityAttribute> ids()
      Return an iterator over the set of primary key names.
      Returns:
      Iterator

    • setter

      Method setter(String name)
      Return the setter method corresponding to this column name.
      Parameters:
      name - Name of the table column
      Returns:
      Method

    • getter

      Method getter(String name)
      Return the getter method corresponding to this column name.
      Parameters:
      name - Name of the table column
      Returns:
      Method

    • pkGetter

      Method pkGetter(String name)
      Return the getter method corresponding to this field name of a primary key.
      Parameters:
      name - Name of the java key field name
      Returns:
      Method

    • oneToOne

      List<RelAttribute> oneToOne()
      Return the attribute for oneToOne mapping field.
      Returns:
      List

    • oneToMany

      List<RelAttribute> oneToMany()
      Return the attribute for oneToMany mapping field.
      Returns:
      List

    • manyToOne

      List<RelAttribute> manyToOne()
      Return the attribute for manyToOne mapping field.
      Returns:
      List

    • relation

      Return the relation for this type and attribute name.
      Parameters:

      name -
      Returns:
